nodes.context: Criminal organization led by Năm Cam, operating in Saigon (Ho Chi Minh City), involved in gambling, racketeering, extortion, loan sharking, and murder.
Female gangster Dung Hà's criminal group from Haiphong, attempted to establish presence in Saigon.
Triad group in Saigon led by Huỳnh Tỳ, initiated Năm Cam into the criminal underworld.
Criminal organization led by Đại Cathay, considered the top of the 'Four Great Kings' of Saigon in the 1960s.
Criminal group operating a casino, led by Bảy Sy, Năm Cam's brother-in-law.
Gang led by Tài chém in the first district of Saigon, targeted by Năm Cam.
Criminal group led by Lê Ngọc Lâm, rival of Năm Cam.

edges.context: Năm Cam and Dung Hà were rival gang leaders; conflict led to Dung Hà's assassination ordered by Năm Cam.
Năm Cam was initiated into the criminal underworld by Huỳnh Tỳ, indicating a subordinate or mentorship relationship.
Năm Cam was a follower of Đại Cathay, learning casino operations from him.
Năm Cam helped run Bảy Sy's casino after Bảy Sy's release from prison.
Năm Cam planned to kill Tài chém, leading to a chase by Tài chém's gang members.
Năm Cam ordered an acid attack on Lê Ngọc Lâm due to underworld friction.
Dung Hà organized the acid attack on Lê Ngọc Lâm on behalf of Năm Cam.
